name: nbconvert (Jupyter Notebook Format Converter)

performance.notes: Conversion speed depends on notebook size and format (PDF slower)

agent_readiness.notes: Convert Jupyter notebooks to other formats. jupyter nbconvert --to html notebook.ipynb. jupyter nbconvert --to pdf notebook.ipynb (requires LaTeX). jupyter nbconvert --to script notebook.ipynb (extract Python). jupyter nbconvert --to markdown notebook.ipynb. jupyter nbconvert --execute --to html notebook.ipynb (execute and convert). Python API: from nbconvert import HTMLExporter. Jinja2 template customization. CI/CD: generate HTML reports from notebook runs. Quarto supersedes for advanced publishing.

gotchas: HTML output includes all raw output (large files for data-heavy notebooks)
Quarto provides more polished publishing output
best_when: CI/CD: execute notebook and generate HTML report
Converting notebooks to Python scripts for code extraction
Quick HTML sharing of Jupyter analysis results
avoid_when: PDF without LaTeX dependency (use Quarto or WeasyPrint)
Interactive notebook sharing (use JupyterHub or nbviewer)
summary: nbconvert converts Jupyter notebooks to HTML, PDF, Markdown, Python scripts, LaTeX. jupyter nbconvert --to html notebook.ipynb or --execute --to html for executed output. CI/CD notebook report generation. Python API via HTMLExporter/PDFExporter. Template customization via Jinja2. PDF requires LaTeX. Use Quarto for publication-quality output, nbconvert for simple format conversion.
